channel.title: Jira on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Jira on RaihnForge

item.title: Internship Week 03: Leveraging Technology
item.description:
1. Technology Usage

At the EdTech employer, technology plays a pivotal role in optimizing daily operations and enhancing workflow efficiency across teams. Three specific examples of tools and systems in use:

Jira (Atlassian)

Jira is the central tool for project tracking and task management. It enables our team to assign, prioritize, and monitor tasks within sprint cycles. This structure improves team coordination, reduces task redundancy, and increases transparency across cross-functional projects, especially during weekly sprint planning and retrospectives.

item.title: ITAM Internship A Reflection
item.description:
1. Learning Objectives

My first learning objective was to gain experience as an Agile project-management scrum master for the development team. Though I assist in prioritization and help unblock the development team throughout sprints, our team does not ascribe to strict scrum implementation. I also prefer to avoid the scrum points system, as it creates a distraction and an alluring incentive for team members to prioritize the gamified system over the actual applied outcomes. My objective was modified to increase confidence and organization during developer daily standups and bi-weekly meetings. I incorporated weekly one-on-one meetings with individual developers in order to assess and assist with progress and prioritization.

item.title: Internship Week 02: Contribution to the Company
item.description:
1. Contribution to the Company

As a project manager at the EdTech employer, I oversee SOC 2 Type 2 certification and lead a website redesign initiative. The SOC 2 Type 2 work is moving slowly but ramping up as we commit to audit dates in collaboration with our SOC 2 auditor. The website redesign has entered phase two. Phase one’s deliverable was confirming that the current site was up to date, compliant, and information-accurate. The redesign itself requires graphic-design collaboration with our design team, and because I have a graphic-design background I was chosen to execute it. The work is being done in Figma, a new tool for me, since past web designs only required Adobe Photoshop. I continue to work on these initiatives, though I have not yet been able to commit the time and attention I would prefer.
